channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: All Blacks secure dominant victory over Ireland in Eden Park
item.description: New Zealand's All Blacks delivered a commanding performance, defeating Ireland 40-21 in a Rugby Nations Championship match at Eden Park. The hosts showcased attacking brilliance, scoring six tries to ...

item.title: Meralco Dominates in PBA Game, Extending Rain or Shine's Losing Streak
item.description: In a pivotal PBA Season 50 Governors’ Cup matchup, Meralco extended their dominance over Rain or Shine, deepening the latter’s struggles in the tournament. The game, played on July 18, 2026, saw Meral...

item.title: Website blocks crawler bot access, urges users to check settings
item.description: The article discusses a technical issue where users are being blocked from accessing News Corp Australia's website due to automated crawler bot activity. The message explains that the site uses softwa...

item.title: Olivia Attwood showcases her glamorous outfit at a high-profile event with the help of her stylist
item.description: The article details Olivia Attwood's preparation for a glamorous event, highlighting her choice of attire featuring a pink lace bra and nude thong. Her stylist is seen assisting her in getting ready, ...

item.title: Rising Child Drownings: Causes and Preventive Measures
item.description: The article highlights the concerning trend of increasing drownings among young children, despite unclear causes. It emphasizes the importance of parental vigilance, water safety education, and commun...

item.title: 25-year-old woman arrested for killing husband's mistress' infant in Benue
item.description: A 25-year-old woman, Erdoo Ugede, has been arrested for allegedly killing the two-month-old son of her husband’s alleged mistress in Ankyoov, Benue State. The incident occurred after she invited the c...

item.title: Piastri's Mechanical Struggles and Ferrari's Race Highlights at Spa
item.description: Oscar Piastri's Formula 1 weekend at Spa-Francorchamps was marked by mechanical woes and Ferrari's heroic efforts to salvage the situation. The McLaren driver faced a hydraulic leak during FP1, requir...

item.title: Uganda's President Honours Late General Moses Ali for His Service and Leadership
item.description: President Yoweri Museveni has paid tribute to General Moses Ali, a veteran Ugandan politician and military leader who passed away at 87. Ali, who served as an MP for Adjumani West and held high-rankin...

item.title: Imo State Governor Credits Tinubu’s Reforms for Boosting Revenue and Debt Reduction
item.description: Hope Uzodimma, governor of Imo State, has praised President Bola Tinubu’s economic reforms for significantly boosting the state’s finances. He highlighted that Imo’s monthly allocation from the Federa...

item.title: Norway's Largest Residential Fire in Modern History Destroys Over 100 Homes
item.description: A residential fire in southern Norway has become the largest in the country's modern history, destroying more than 100 homes and forcing hundreds of residents to evacuate. The fire, which began in a t...

item.title: FIFA president travels over 57,000 miles across three host nations for World Cup
item.description: The FIFA president, Gianni Infantino, has flown approximately 57,700 miles between Canada, Mexico, and the U.S. during this year’s World Cup, which features 104 matches. His luxury Gulfstream G650ER j...

item.title: WHO Releases Guidelines on Preventing Dementia Through Lifestyle and Environmental Interventions
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has released updated guidelines emphasizing the importance of modifiable lifestyle and environmental factors in preventing dementia. The report highlights that up t...

item.title: Elderly Tanzanian man marries son's ex-wife amid cultural controversy
item.description: An 86-year-old Tanzanian man, Mr Klarensi Usakazi, has married his son's former wife, Mary, 20, after her parents failed to refund the dowry. The polygamist claims the union prevents loss of his custo...

item.title: Cambodia Advances to SEA V Cup Final After Victory Over Thailand
item.description: In a stunning upset, the Cambodian volleyball team secured their place in the SEA V Cup final after defeating Thailand in a thrilling match held in Candon, Ilocos Sur. The victory, which took place on...

item.title: CBSE Class 10 Second Board 2026 Results Declared: Steps to Download Marksheet and Key Statistics
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has released the results for the Class 10 Second Board Examination 2026. Students can access their digital marksheet through DigiLocker by following spe...

item.title: Ottawa's Data Centre Expansion and Environmental Concerns
item.description: The Canadian federal government is advancing its AI strategy by promoting the construction of large-scale data centres, which are critical for supporting artificial intelligence initiatives. Ottawa ha...

item.title: ODM Holds Meetings at State House as Ruling Party
item.description: Junet Mohamed, Suna East MP, has confirmed that the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) holds meetings at State House, framing it as proof the party is now part of the government rather than the oppositi...

item.title: US military casualties in Jordan amid Iranian strikes
item.description: Two U.S. service members were killed and one is missing in action following Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks in Jordan, according to a statement from U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM). The inc...

item.title: Collingwood extends winning streak to five with 21-point victory over Carlton as Josh Daicos battles through an ankle injury
item.description: Collingwood continued its strong late-season form with a 14.6 (90) to 10.9 (69) victory over Carlton at the MCG, recording a fifth consecutive AFL win and strengthening its prospects of securing at le...

item.title: Former San Beda Basketball Player Dies in Car Accident
item.description: Former San Beda Red Lion and Chiang Kai Shek College standout John Vincent 'JV' Gallego died on July 18, 2026, at the age of 28 after a road accident. Gallego was driving back to Manila on the North L...
